adds.rationale: NUKZ launched January 23, 2024 and provides nuclear-renaissance exposure URA does not — utilities running reactors, reactor developers, and construction/services alongside uranium fuel — entering as uranium spot hit a 16-year high and AI-data-center baseload demand begins lifting the nuclear thesis.

news_evidence.summary: NUKZ launched Jan 23, 2024, tracking the VettaFi Nuclear Renaissance Index (53 holdings across reactors, utilities, construction/services, fuel) — broader than URA's uranium-mining-only focus.
Uranium spot hit a 16-year high of $106/lb in late January 2024 on Kazatomprom production-miss news; held near $85/lb through late March.

adds.rationale: Scorpio Tankers is the cleanest US-listed product-tanker pure-play for the Red Sea disruption trade: Houthi attacks since November 2023 rerouted ships via the Cape of Good Hope, shrinking effective tanker supply and lifting freight rates, while STNG's Q4 2023 earnings (Feb 14) beat on revenue and EPS.

news_evidence.summary: S&P Global: tighter fleet capacity, Red Sea disruptions, and Cape rerouting expected to boost 2024 tanker freight; net product-tanker fleet growth just 1.4% in 2024.
Scorpio Tankers Q4 2023 (Feb 14, 2024): revenue $334.07M, net income $120.9M ($2.43 EPS), beating estimates; owns/leases 110 tankers.

removes.rationale: BOTZ's tilt toward Japanese industrial-automation incumbents (FANUC, Keyence, ABB) provides negligible exposure to the humanoid-robot buildup the profile names; the Q1 2024 AI rally is driven by pure semiconductor/software names, and no new milestone re-anchored BOTZ to the thesis.

news_evidence.summary: Global X BOTZ concentrates in Japan-listed industrial-automation incumbents (FANUC, Keyence dominant); top five ~40% of the fund — limited humanoid exposure.
Q1 2024 best-performing ETFs were semiconductor/AI-infrastructure funds, confirming the rally favored pure AI/semis over the industrial automation that dominates BOTZ.

removes.rationale: GOOGL's AI execution stumbled in February 2024 when Google paused Gemini image generation after racially inaccurate outputs, with Pichai calling the results 'completely unacceptable'; with MSFT and NVDA already covering AI strongly, a third AI name adds diminishing diversification and the slot is better redeployed.

news_evidence.summary: Google paused Gemini's AI image generation on Feb 22, 2024 after historically inaccurate racial depictions.
Pichai (Feb 28, 2024): Gemini's responses 'offended our users and showed bias... completely unacceptable and we got it wrong.'

rationale_overall: The Q1 2024 rebalance narrows AI from three names to two (keeping MSFT and NVDA, the strongest execution stories) while rotating the freed slot plus the BOTZ slot into two uncovered thesis gaps: geopolitical/tanker (STNG, on the active Red Sea disruption) and a broader nuclear-renaissance play (NUKZ, capturing utilities and reactor developers URA misses). Post-change spans AI (MSFT, NVDA), rockets (RKLB), quantum (QTUM), nuclear (URA, NUKZ), defense (ITA), geopolitical (STNG) — holding the current AI wave while seeding the next waves early.
